articles.title: investingLive European markets wrap: Dollar, oil prices drop further on US-Iran hope
articles.expand: Headlines: Gold rebounds strongly as hope for the end of the war returns. What's next? US president Trump continues to gas up the stock market, posts Dow snapshot The Bank of Japan is back in the yen market China keeps up with the gold buying spree as reserves climb for a 18th straight month ECB's Nagel: The ECB is likely to hike rates unless the o...

articles.title: GBP/USD Price Forecast: Advancing 20-day EMA supports upside towards 1.3700
articles.expand: The GBP/USD pair trades 0.18% higher around 1.3620 during the European trading session on Thursday. The Cable reflects strength as the US Dollar (USD) faces selling pressure amid the optimism that the United States (US) and Iran will reach a peace deal soon.

articles.title: Euro stands tall as hopes of a swift end to Iran's war boost risk appetite
articles.expand: The Euro (EUR) appreciates against the US Dollar (USD) for the second consecutive day on Thursday, trading at 1.1765 at the time of writing, fuelled by hopes of a peace deal that would end Iran’s war and upbeat Eurozone macroeconomic releases.

articles.title: Gold: Pullback before potential $5,200 breakout – TD Securities
articles.expand: TD Securities strategist Bart Melek argues that Gold’s recent decline reflects the Iran-driven Oil shock, higher inflation expectations and a firmer US Dollar, which are keeping Fed policy tighter for longer.

articles.title: NZD/USD approaches 0.6000 as hopes of a US-Iran peace deal boost risk appetite
articles.expand: The New Zealand Dollar (NZD) keeps marching higher at two-month highs against the US Dollar (USD) on Thursday, as reports of progress in the US-Iran peace deal and lower Oil prices keep demand for the safe-haven US Dollar subdued.

articles.title: DXY: Range-bound outlook after war reversal – BBH
articles.expand: Brown Brothers Harriman’s (BBH) Elias Haddad notes that the Dollar has given back all its war-related gains, with recent weakness seen as overdone.

articles.title: Fed’s Collins: Expect rates to be on hold for longer
articles.expand: Boston Federal Reserve (Fed) Bank President Susan Collins said during the late European trading session on Thursday that she sees the central bank holding interest rates at their current levels for longer. Collins added that she expects interest rate cuts to be the baseline scenario going ahead.

articles.title: Fed's Collins: I still expect interest rate cuts down the road
articles.expand: I preferred to adjust wording that signals cutting bias I still expect rate cuts down the road Rates will likely remain on hold for a longer period The odds of worse inflation scenario have increased Alternative scenario could make the Fed consider a hike Fed's Collins is not a voter this year, so we haven't got the chance to see her dissent regard...

articles.title: USD/INR corrects further as US-Iran optimism pushes oil prices lower
articles.expand: The Indian Rupee (INR) recovers early losses and turns positive against the US Dollar (USD) on Thursday.

articles.title: Euro holds gains amid US-Iran peace hopes, better-than-expected Eurozone data
articles.expand: The Euro (EUR) appreciates against the US Dollar (USD) for the second consecutive day on Thursday, trading at 1.1765 at the time of writing, fuelled by hopes of a peace deal that would end Iran’s war and upbeat Eurozone macroeconomic releases.

articles.title: EUR/SEK: Rebound tests key moving average – Societe Generale
articles.expand: Societe Generale analysts note that the central bank of Sweden, Riksbank left rates on hold this morning at 1.75% and EUR/SEK has been rebounding from an interim low and is now challenging its 200-day moving average, which has capped the pair since last year.

articles.title: Asian FX: Relief rally and differentiation theme – OCBC
articles.expand: OCBC strategists Sim Moh Siong and Christopher Wong characterize recent moves in Asian FX as a relief rally driven by lower Oil and hopes of a US–Iran deal. They stress that any agreement and normalization of flows through the Strait of Hormuz remain uncertain.

articles.title: AUD/USD: Break above 0.72 highlights risk-on rotation – DBS
articles.expand: Philip Wee at DBS Group Research points out that the Australian Dollar (AUD) has been a key beneficiary of the risk-on shift. AUD/USD broke above 0.72 after the Reserve Bank of Australia (RBA) signalled a pause following three consecutive rate hikes.

articles.title: AUD/USD Price Forecast: Bullish near multi-year top amid Iran peace deal hopes, hawkish RBA
articles.expand: The AUD/USD pair attracts some buyers for the third consecutive day on Thursday and remains within striking distance of its highest level since June 2022, around the 0.7275-0.7280 region, which it touched the previous day.

articles.title: USD: Softer tone as Fed seen delaying cuts – MUFG
articles.expand: MUFG’s Lloyd Chan notes the US Dollar (USD) has softened as US Dollar Index (DXY) retests support and US yields fall, while equities hit new highs. Recent US data show stronger ADP employment but worrying ISM services inflation and weak employment.

articles.title: WTI Oil dips to levels approaching $90.00 amid Hormuz opening rumours
articles.expand: Crude Oil prices extend losses for the third consecutive day on Thursday, with the US benchmark West Texas Intermediate (WTI) trading at $90.66 at the time of writing, as progress in the US-Iran peace talks has sparked speculation about the reopening of the Strait of Hormuz.

articles.title: ECB’s Villeroy: Next move should be guided by the data, not the date
articles.expand: European Central Bank (ECB) policymaker and Bank of France Governor Francois Villeroy de Galhau said in the European trading session on Thursday that the central bank should focus on the data and not the timing of any monetary policy adjustment

articles.title: Euro area: Energy shock rekindles price pressures – Societe Generale
articles.expand: Societe Generale economists Sam Cartwright, Michel Martinez and Jorge Garayo analyse Euro Area inflation following the latest energy shock. They note headline inflation has already risen to 3% and expect indirect and second‑round effects to push it toward 3.5% in early 2027.

articles.title: EUR/CZK: Sideways despite hawkish CNB risk – Commerzbank
articles.expand: Commerzbank’s Tatha Ghose expects the Czech National Bank (CNB) to keep rates at 3.50% but sees upside inflation risks from higher energy prices and a growing chance of at least one 25 bp hike later in 2026.

articles.title: USDMXN Intraday Flashback: From ABC Correction to Bearish Impulse
articles.expand: A step-by-step look at how USDMXN completed an ABC corrective recovery, reacted at projected resistance, broke below confirmation support, and transitioned into a new bearish impulsive decline.

articles.title: ECB policymaker Villeroy urges against speculating on timing of potential rate hike
articles.expand: ECB's François Villeroy, Governor of the Banque de France, cautioned against premature speculation regarding the timing of future interest rate hikes. Villeroy has been emphasising that the ECB must remain "data-driven, not date-driven". He recently said that the ECB needs "critical mass of data" before considering rate hikes and added that any tig...

articles.title: What are the main events for today?
articles.expand: EUROPEAN SESSION In the European session, we don't have much on the agenda other than a few low tier releases like the French trade balance, construction PMIs and Eurozone retail sales. The data won't change anything for the ECB, so the market reaction will likely be muted. The focus today will be on Iran as it's expected to deliver a response via ...

articles.title: AUD/CAD Targeting Parity as Peace Optimism, Falling Oil and RBA-BoC Divergence Align
articles.expand: AUD/CAD is rapidly moving toward parity as multiple macro forces suddenly align in favor of the Aussie at the same time. The shift from Middle East war fears toward peace optimism is simultaneously boosting risk appetite globally while pressuring oil prices lower—a rare combination that strongly favors AUD over CAD. The market narrative changed dra...

articles.title: US senator teases Boeing airplane purchases when Trump and Xi meet next week
articles.expand: As a reminder, US president Trump is scheduled to meet with China president Xi Jinping next week on 14-15 May. The meeting will take place in Beijing and will mark the first time since a US president has travelled to China since 2017. There will be a lot of things to talk about and we're already starting to see the groundwork get laid out this week...

articles.title: Macklem Warns Energy Shock Could Force Consecutive BoC Hikes
articles.expand: BoC Governor Tiff Macklem delivered a clear warning that the Bank of Canada is becoming increasingly uncomfortable with the inflation risks created by the Middle East conflict. While policymakers are still willing to “look through” the first-round jump in gasoline prices, Macklem stressed that the BoC “will not let” higher energy costs evolve into ...

articles.title: Event Guide: Canada’s Employment Report (April 2026)
articles.expand: With the Bank of Canada (BOC) still holding a balancing act between strong inflation and weak growth, will the upcoming jobs report tip the scales in a clearer direction?

articles.title: Most S’pore home owners still prefer fixed home loans although floating rates now cheaper
articles.expand: Reasons cited include stability in monthly repayments and to hedge against rate hikes.

articles.title: Retail Giants Pivot to 'Masstige' to Capture 290bn Baht Beauty Boom
articles.expand: Thailand’s retail conglomerates are spinning off beauty divisions to target Gen Z and cost-conscious consumers, leveraging global sourcing to disrupt unofficial imports

articles.title: Bessent to raise weak yen with Japanese officials in Tokyo meetings next week
articles.expand: U.S. Treasury Secretary Bessent will visit Japan next week for talks covering the weak yen, rare earths and energy procurement, with individual meetings scheduled with the PM, finance minister and Bank of Japan governor, the Nikkei reports. articles.title: Stock markets roar back as oil sinks below $100 amid fresh optimism over an Iran war peace deal
articles.expand: The FTSE 100 surged 219.55 points, or 2.15%, to 10,438.7 - enjoying its best day in a month - as miners, aviation stocks and banks raced ahead.

articles.expand: BitcoinWorld USD/CHF Slides for Second Day as Risk Appetite Returns and Swiss Inflation Firms The Swiss franc continued to strengthen against the US dollar for a second consecutive trading session on Tuesday, as an improvement in global risk sentiment and a firmer-than-expected Swiss inflation reading weighed on the greenback. The USD/CHF pair edge...

articles.title: GBPUSD backs off into support. Yesterday's resistance is today's support.
articles.expand: The GBPUSD moved higher yesterday, but upside momentum stalled near the lower end of a key swing area resistance zone between 1.3574 and 1.3602. The rally peaked at 1.3577 before sellers leaned against the resistance area and pushed the pair lower into the new trading day, sending the price back toward its 100- and 200-hour moving averages. During ...

articles.title: AUDUSD runs to a new high going back to 2022 & backs off.What keeps the buyers in control?
articles.expand: The AUDUSD extended higher after the Reserve Bank of Australia raised rates by 25 basis points yesterday, effectively reversing the rate cuts seen earlier in 2025. The move marked the central bank’s third consecutive hike as policymakers continue their fight against stubborn inflation pressures. Technically, the bullish momentum gained traction yes...

articles.title: Eurozone PMI Falls Into Contraction as Energy Shock Hits Services Hard
articles.expand: Eurozone private sector activity slipped back into contraction in April, with the Services PMI finalized at 47.6, down sharply from 50.2 in March and marking a 62-month low. Composite PMI fell from 50.7 to 48.8, its weakest reading in 17 months and the first contractionary print in nearly a year and a half. The data […] The post Eurozone PMI ...

articles.title: Asia open: Equities hit records as geopolitical tensions cool and oil dips
articles.expand: Global markets surged as easing US–Iran tensions and softer oil prices lifted risk appetite, driving the S P 500 and Nasdaq to record highs. Strong earnings momentum, led by mega-cap tech and the AI infrastructure boom, continues to underpin equities. The Australian dollar rebounded on hawkish RBA guidance, while a cooling geopolitical risk premium...

articles.title: NAB calls June RBA hike to 4.60% as Middle East inflation compounds domestic pressures
articles.expand: National Australia Bank now expects the RBA to hike again in June to 4.60%, arguing the central bank faces a compounding inflation shock it cannot afford to let run, with cuts pencilled in for H2 2027. Earlier: ING sees AUD rebound ahead as RBA signals pause but stands ready to act Westpac sees upside inflation risks after RBA lifts cash rate to 4....

articles.title: NZ Labor Data Gives RBNZ Room to Stay on Hold
articles.expand: New Zealand’s labor market showed tentative signs of stabilization in the first quarter, with the unemployment rate unexpectedly falling from 5.4% to 5.3%, below expectations for no change. Employment rose 0.2% qoq, slightly under the expected 0.3% gain, marking modest improvement. However, the decline in unemployment was not entirely driven by str...

articles.title: ING sees AUD rebound ahead as RBA signals pause but stands ready to act
articles.expand: ING expects the Australian dollar to recover after post-RBA weakness, saying the central bank retains its hawkish credentials and stands ready to hike again if inflation data materially surprises. Earlier: Westpac sees upside inflation risks after RBA lifts cash rate to 4.35% in 8-1 vote AUD faces headwinds above 0.72 as RBA signals pause, TD warns...

articles.title: CBA sees RBA on hold for rest of 2026 after third consecutive hike to 4.35%
articles.expand: Commonwealth Bank expects the RBA to hold rates at 4.35% for the rest of 2026 before cutting in 2027, though an August hike remains possible if Q2 trimmed mean CPI surprises to the upside. Earlier: Westpac sees upside inflation risks after RBA lifts cash rate to 4.35% in 8-1 vote AUD faces headwinds above 0.72 as RBA signals pause, TD warns Summary...

articles.title: AUD faces headwinds above 0.72 as RBA signals pause, TD warns
articles.expand: TD Securities has shifted its RBA call to a final 25bps hike in August to 4.60%, citing dovish May messaging and Middle East income shock risks, while warning AUD faces near-term headwinds above 0.72. Summary: The RBA hiked the cash rate 25 basis points to 4.35% in an 8-1 vote at its May meeting, with messaging characterised as dovish and pointing ...

articles.title: Westpac sees upside inflation risks after RBA lifts cash rate to 4.35% in 8-1 vote
articles.expand: The RBA raised its cash rate 25bps to 4.35% in an 8-1 vote, citing Middle East inflation pressures, but Westpac sees the June move as more finely balanced after Governor Bullock's dovish tone. Summary: The RBA Monetary Policy Board raised the cash rate by 25 basis points to 4.35% at its May 2026 meeting in an 8-1 vote, up from a 5-4 split in March,...

articles.title: RBNZ says financial system resilient but Middle East conflict to slow recovery
articles.expand: New Zealand's central bank says its financial system is resilient and banks can withstand significant shocks, but warns the Middle East conflict will slow the country's economic recovery. Summary: The RBNZ's May 2026 Financial Stability Report found the financial system resilient and well positioned to support households and businesses even if cond...

articles.title: UK 30-year gilts hit 5.78%, the highest since 1998: what is being priced in?
articles.expand: UK 30-year gilts traded as high as 5.78% on Tuesday, the highest level since 1998, while 10-year yields topped 5.10% with markets pricing in nearly three-quarter-point Bank of England (BoE) rate hikes this year.

articles.title: USDJPY buyers make a play with the break of a key MA. The EURUSD/GBPUSD are steady.
articles.expand: The USDJPY tumbled sharply last week after intervention fears out of Japan surfaced as the pair pushed above the 160.00 level. That warning triggered aggressive selling, driving the price down to 155.46—just ahead of the 61.8% retracement of the move up from the February 12 low (155.505). Buyers leaned against that level, and the pair began to rota...

articles.title: Swiss CPI Accelerates to 0.6% YoY as Energy Import Costs Rebound
articles.expand: Swiss inflation picked up modestly in April, with headline CPI rising 0.3% month-on-month, driven largely by higher energy and travel-related costs. According to the Federal Statistical Office, increases in petrol, diesel and heating oil prices were key contributors, alongside higher airfares and international package holidays. The underlying pictu...

articles.title: War fears tarnish Metals – Silver (XAG/USD) breaks $75 & Gold (XAU/USD) tests $4,500
articles.expand: XAU/USD, XAG/USD Outlook: Precious metals decline amid escalating Middle East tensions and surging inflation worries. As gold and silver break lower, traders continue to question their role as safe havens in a tense environment. The action gets more bearish in this new week. Explore an intraday technical analysis of XAU/USD and XAG/USD.

articles.title: Oil Shock + Hawkish Fed = Major EUR/USD Downside? Don't Miss These Trading Opportunities
articles.expand: Pressure is building fast on EUR/USD — and two powerful macro forces could send it lower in May. Higher oil prices from Strait of Hormuz disruptions are hammering European growth… while a potentially more hawkish Fed under Kevin Warsh is supercharging the US Dollar. Bearish EUR/USD isn't just a trade on Europe — it's a broad bet on dollar strength....
